title: Llama7B dense tile reduction/NoC frontier
hypothesis: With dense_gemm_16x8_k1_p1 fixed as the measured compute anchor, the Llama7B 131k frontier should be governed by cross-tile reduction, global NoC bandwidth/hops, and SRAM placement rather than by raw GEMM tile PPA alone.

direct_comparison.primary_question: For the selected dense tile compute anchor, which local/shared SRAM, cluster count, NoC bandwidth/hop, and reduction-overhead points remain on the Llama7B frontier?
include: NoC bandwidth 4096 through 65536 bytes/cycle and hop counts 1, 2, 4, and 8
exclude: quality-unbacked KV reductions such as KV4 or MQA
changing the measured local L1 profile family during this focused pass
follow_on_broad_sweep: build or calibrate a more detailed SRAM/global NoC simulator around the winning region

expected_benefit: classify whether the current frontier is compute-bound, reduction-bound, shared-SRAM/NoC-bound, or HBM-sensitive
identify the memory/NoC architecture region worth embodying next
avoid spending synthesis budget on more dense compute until the communication limiter is clearer
risks: global SRAM/NoC arbitration is still analytic rather than cycle-accurate
command and reduction overhead knobs are sensitivity terms, not measured routed hierarchy PPA
filtering to one measured L1 profile may hide profile-family sensitivity

expected_result.reason: The result should rank dense_gemm_16x8_k1_p1 schedule points by SRAM placement, NoC bandwidth/hops, cluster count, and reduction overhead so the next embodied memory/NoC design target is clear.
